description: Autograph letter from Avraham Maimonides (r. 1205–1237 CE) to his "dear son" (i.e., congenial pupil) Yosef. In Judaeo-Arabic. Avraham excuses his absence from a student's recent wedding, saying, "The night of your wedding was the night of my turn to be on duty at the hospital, and I could not at that time put it off for reasons it would take too long to explain" (translation from Mark Cohen, “The Burdensome Life of a Jewish Physician and Communal Leader,” p. 135).
description_he: מכתב אוטוגרף מאברהם מיימוני לתלמידו אהובו יוסף ביהודית-ערבית. מסביר מדוע נעדר מחתונת התלמיד: לילה ההוא היה תורנותו בבית החולים.
